mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below 0.1 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust household purchasing power without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, which sustains equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative cumulative return over 60 trading days in more than 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during a post-recession recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and rising business investment, which generates immediate positive market sentiment and drives S&P 500 gains over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in over 50% of recovery-phase observations, or the signal predictive power vanishes when tested outside the specified recovery regime.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates expanding economic activity and rising wage bills, which supports corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal produces a negative 20-day return in more than 55% of cases, or the rank correlation with forward returns drops below 0.01 across all tested sub-periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase exceeding 0.4 percent signals accelerating inflation that forces central banks to adopt restrictive monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of high-inflation trigger events, or the inflation threshold fails to separate winning and losing periods with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and housing starts confirms broad-based economic strength and rising household wealth effects, which boosts consumer confidence and equity demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and housing signal yields negative 20-day returns in over 55% of instances, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising federal funds rates indicates a manufacturing contraction under tightening financial conditions, which triggers profit warnings and risk-off sentiment, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of tightening-cycle observations, or the joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 3-year walk-forward test.
